---
title: "AgentCOP"
subtitle: "Keeping proprietary data off the cloud — a local policy gate + redaction loop"
date: 2026-06-06
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: night
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    incremental: false
    code-line-numbers: false
    mermaid:
      theme: dark
---

## The problem {.smaller}

Frontier cloud models are powerful — but a lot of the data we'd send them **can't leave the building**:

- 🏥 **PHI** — patient names, MRNs, dates (HIPAA)
- 🏭 **OT secrets** — controller network config, credentials, robot programs
- 🔒 **Trade secrets & export-controlled** data

::: {.fragment}
**Goal:** get frontier-model answers *without* leaking the sensitive bits.

The idea: try a small **local** model first; only escalate to the cloud after a gate
confirms (or makes) the data safe to send.
:::

## The full flow

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Prompt + Box docs] --> B[Local LLM<br/>draft answer]
    B --> C{Hallucination<br/>check}
    C -- trustworthy --> D[Return answer · STOP]
    C -- escalate --> E[POLICY AGENT]
    E --> F{Proprietary?}
    F -- no --> G[Send to cloud · STOP]
    F -- uncertain --> H[Human review]
    F -- yes --> I[Redactor]
    I -- re-check --> E
    style E fill:#1b5e20,stroke:#4caf50,color:#fff
    style I fill:#4a148c,stroke:#ba68c8,color:#fff
```

::: {.fragment}
Four components, four owners — chained into one pipeline (`orchestrator.py`).
:::

## The components {.smaller}

| Stage | Does | Built by |
|------|------|----------|
| **Box + local LLM** | Pulls relevant files, drafts an answer locally | Zack |
| **Hallucination check** | Is the local answer trustworthy? | Abhiyan & Nav |
| **Policy Agent** | Decides if data may go to the cloud — *this talk* | Ammar |
| **Redactor** | Removes the sensitive bits, proves it (fail-closed) | Emma |

::: {.fragment}
Only data the local model *can't* handle ever reaches the gate — and only **clean** data ever reaches the cloud.
:::

## The Policy Agent — the gate {.smaller}

Runs **locally**, two stages:

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    I[prompt + data] --> C[1 · Classify<br/>which case?]
    C --> E[2 · Evaluate<br/>apply that policy]
    E --> V{verdict}
    V --> A[ALLOW → cloud]
    V --> B[BLOCK → redactor]
    V --> H[ESCALATE → human]
```

- **Modular policies** per use-case (`healthcare`, `industrial_ot`) + extensible registry
- **3-state** verdict — never a silent leak; when unsure → **human**
- A deterministic guard escalates anything it *can't even read* (opaque/binary)

## Structured output → drives redaction {.smaller}

The gate doesn't just say "no" — it says **what, where, and how to fix**:

```json
{
  "verdict": "BLOCK",
  "matched_case": "healthcare",
  "matched_policy_rules": ["HC-1", "HC-2", "HC-3"],
  "findings": [
    {"category": "phi", "location": "MRN: NL-448821",
     "severity": "high", "redaction_hint": "mask"},
    {"category": "phi", "location": "DOB 09/14/1951",
     "severity": "high", "redaction_hint": "generalize"}
  ]
}
```

This contract is what the **redactor** consumes.

## The redaction loop {.smaller}

On **BLOCK**, the redactor strips PHI (true removal, then re-scans — fail-closed),
and the cleaned doc is **re-checked by the gate** until it passes.

::: {.fragment}
**The catch that made or broke it:** the redactor leaves placeholder tokens
`[PATIENT] [MRN] [DOB] …`. The gate first saw those and said *"PHI detected!"* —
re-blocking the redactor's own output **forever.**
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Fix = a shared contract.** Teach the gate that those tokens mean *already removed* → ALLOW.
That single agreement is what lets the loop **converge**.
:::

## Example 1 — healthcare discharge {.smaller}

**File:** `data/healthcare/01_discharge_heart_failure_Okafor.pdf` · **Prompt:** *"find any follow-ups"*

The gate flagged real PHI (synthetic patient), then the loop cleared it:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="52%"}
| rule | finding |
|---|---|
| HC-1 | name `Okafor, Emmanuel C.` |
| HC-2 | MRN `NL-448821` |
| HC-3 | DOB `09/14/1951` |
| HC-7 | attending `Dr. Priya Mehta` |
:::
::: {.column width="48%"}
```
loop 1: BLOCK (14 findings)
        → redactor: 50 redactions, CLEAR
loop 2: ALLOW
        → SEND_TO_CLOUD (redacted doc)
```
:::
:::

Verdict: `policy-agent/examples/verdicts/` · Redaction audit: `output/…Okafor.audit.json`

## Example 2 — ABB controller backup {.smaller}

**File:** `…/RobotStudio/…/6640_2-14-22_6.05restore/` · **Prompt:** *"check the errors on the log…"*

Intake walked the backup tree and found secrets the policy never hard-coded:

| rule | finding | from |
|---|---|---|
| OT-1 | hostname `DESKTOP-9T2SPJS` | `rsmeta.xml` |
| OT-1 | PROFINET device addresses | `HOME/ippnio.xml` |
| OT-3 | controller serial `6640-501456` | `system.xml` |
| OT-2/3 | UAS grants + key | `uas_application_grants.xml`, `key.id` |

```
verdict: BLOCK  (industrial_ot, 49 findings)  →  no OT massager yet  →  HUMAN_REVIEW
```

## Coverage at a glance {.smaller}

Every case, real run — **9/9 validated**:

| input | verdict | path |
|---|---|---|
| "what is ABB error 50056?" | **ALLOW** → cloud | generic, no attachment |
| de-identified clinical question | **ALLOW** → cloud | no identifiers |
| discharge PDF (PHI) | **BLOCK** → redact → **ALLOW** | `data/healthcare/*.pdf` |
| controller backup | **BLOCK** → human review | `…/6640…restore/` |
| opaque `.bin` attachment | **ESCALATE** → human | unreadable |

Sample machine outputs committed at `policy-agent/examples/verdicts/*.verdict.json`

## Design choices & honest limits {.smaller}

- ✅ **Model-agnostic** — runs on any OpenRouter/Gemini model; default is a small **local-class** 8B
- ⚠️ **Small model over-flags on re-check** (confabulates PHI on cleaned docs) → the loop needs a
  *capable* evaluator for the verification step
- ⚠️ **Redaction is healthcare-only today** → `industrial_ot` BLOCK routes to human review
- ✅ **Fail-safe by construction** — unknown / unreadable / uncertain → human, never a leak

## Takeaways

- A small **local gate** can let you use frontier models **without leaking** sensitive data
- The hard part isn't any one component — it's the **contracts between them**
  (structured findings ↔ redaction tokens)
- **Next:** an `industrial_ot` redactor, a shared confidence/threshold config, one `.env`

::: {.fragment}
**Team:** Zack (Box + local LLM) · Abhiyan & Nav (hallucination check) ·
Ammar (policy agent) · Emma (redaction)
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Thank you** — questions?
:::
